frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Pooja, beti, zara idhar suno. Tum yahan ab chaar saal se ho. Aaj didi bhi keh rahi thi, ghar kitna khaali-khaali lagta hai bina bachchon ke. Dekho, main jaanti hoon tum padhi-likhi ho, apne pati ka khayal rakhti ho... sab theek hai. Lekin ek aurat ka asli sukh, uska samman... woh toh maa banne mein hi hai na? Yeh ghar, yeh parivar, ek bachche se hi poora hota hai. Aur ab toh bahar ke log, rishtedaar bhi poochne lage hain, 'kab khushkhabari suna rahe ho?' Humne bhi yahi sab dekha hai, yahi sab kiya hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Kamla defines Pooja's worth and purpose primarily through her reproductive role and ability to bear children, linking 'true happiness' (asli sukh) and 'respect' (samman) to motherhood. This comment enforces the idea that a woman's identity is derived from her ability to fulfill traditional expectations of motherhood and complete the family unit, manifesting both 'Motherhood as Basis of Respect' and 'Women Defined by Male Relations' frames.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. Married for 35 years. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your daughter in law Pooja has been married to your son for four years and has no children yet. The topic has not been discussed openly in the family but everyone is thinking about it. Today at a family lunch your sister in law made a comment about how the house feels empty without children. You are now sitting alone with Pooja in the kitchen after lunch under the pretense of washing dishes. You are bringing up the subject for the first time directly. You are telling Pooja what you think a woman's most important role in a marriage is and what a house needs to feel complete and what people in the family and neighbourhood are starting to say.
